name: pr-reviewer-mediforce
title: PR Reviewer — Mediforce
description: Pick an open PR (not draft, no comments yet) on Appsilon/mediforce, run the code-review skill on the diff, and post the review as a single comment on the PR. Manual trigger; no schedule.
preamble:
## Context
This workflow audits open PRs on the Appsilon/mediforce repository. Steps 1 and 3 use a GitHub-MCP-bound agent (user-to-server OAuth, scoped to the user's installation) to fetch and post on the PR. Step 2 is sandboxed (no GitHub access) and runs the `code-review` skill on the inline diff carried over from step 1.

steps.name: Find PR to review
steps.description: Use GitHub MCP to find an open PR on Appsilon/mediforce that is not a draft and has no human comments/reviews yet. Output {found: true, pr_number, title, html_url, diff} or {found: false}.

agent.prompt:
## Task

Use your GitHub MCP tools to list open pull requests on `Appsilon/mediforce`. Find the FIRST PR that meets ALL of these criteria:
- `state` is `open`
- not a draft (`draft == false`)
- has no **human** issue comments, review comments, or reviews yet — i.e. the PR has not yet received feedback from a person

## Filtering bot vs human authors

GitHub returns each comment / review with an author object that has a `type` field. Treat as **bot** (and ignore for the purposes of this filter) when EITHER:
- `user.type == "Bot"` — GitHub explicitly labels App-backed accounts this way (this is the authoritative signal)
- `user.login` contains the literal substring `[bot]` (with the brackets — e.g. `vercel[bot]`, `github-actions[bot]`, `dependabot[bot]`, `renovate[bot]`, `coderabbitai[bot]`). Real GitHub usernames cannot contain `[` or `]`, so a literal-bracket suffix is unambiguous. Do NOT match user accounts that happen to end in the substring `bot` without brackets (e.g. `turbot`, `roboto`, `iamrobot` — those are humans).

Also ignore comments authored by **the same OAuth-connected GitHub identity that this workflow is running as** — i.e. the user behind the GitHub MCP installation. (Easiest signal: call `get_me` once and treat any comment/review whose `user.login` matches the result as "self".) This stops the workflow from re-reviewing PRs it already commented on in a previous run.

A PR with ONLY bot comments and/or self comments still counts as "no human review yet" and IS eligible.

## Output

If no eligible PR exists:
```json
{"found": false}
```

Otherwise fetch the diff (use `get_pull_request_diff` or equivalent) and output:
```json
{
  "found": true,
  "pr_number": <number>,
  "title": <string>,
  "html_url": <string>,
  "diff": <unified diff text — keep as a JSON string with newlines preserved>
}
```

Write the JSON object to `${output_dir}/result.json` — that is your only deliverable. Do not call any tools beyond the GitHub MCP toolset, and do not produce a chat-style summary.

steps.name: No PR to review
steps.description: No eligible PR found — terminate the run.

steps.name: Code review
steps.description: Run the code-review skill on the inline diff produced by step `find-pr`. Sandboxed — no MCP / GitHub access. Output a ready-to-post review comment.

agent.prompt:
## Inputs

The pull request being reviewed is described under `## Previous Step Outputs` → `find-pr`. Fields available:
- `pr_number`, `title`, `html_url`
- `diff`: the unified diff to review

The `code-review` skill text above explains the review structure and rubric.

## Output

Apply the skill to the diff. Produce a single JSON object with one field:
```json
{ "review_comment": <markdown string> }
```
where `review_comment` is the formatted review ready to be posted as a single GitHub PR comment.

**Required header.** The first line of `review_comment` must be exactly:

```
> 🤖 Automated review by **Mediforce Code Review Agent** — flag false positives so we can tune the prompt.
```

Followed by a blank line, then the actual review (Summary / Findings / Verdict from the skill rubric). The header is a Markdown blockquote so GitHub renders it visually distinct from the review body. Keep it on a single line — concise, no extra paragraphs.

Write the JSON to `${output_dir}/result.json`. Do not call any external tools — work only from the diff already provided in the prompt.

steps.name: Post review comment
steps.description: Post the review comment from the review step on the PR found in find-pr.

agent.prompt:
## Task

Use your GitHub MCP tools to post a single comment on the pull request identified in step `find-pr`.

- Repo: `Appsilon/mediforce`
- PR number: from `## Previous Step Outputs` → `find-pr.pr_number`
- Comment body: from `## Previous Step Outputs` → `review.review_comment`

Call `add_issue_comment` (or the equivalent MCP tool) with `owner="Appsilon"`, `repo="mediforce"`, `issue_number=<pr_number>`, `body=<review_comment>`.

After the comment is posted, write `{"posted": true, "comment_url": <url returned by MCP>}` to `${output_dir}/result.json`. No additional commentary.
